skill.name: opentrons-integration
skill.description: Create Opentrons Protocol API v2 protocols for Flex and OT-2 robots, including pipetting, labware setup, hardware modules, PCR workflows, and serial dilutions.
skill.summary: Build Opentrons Flex and OT-2 liquid handling protocols with practical templates and API guidance.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but manual review found false positives from markdown code fences and Opentrons API names such as load_labware, transfer, home, and PCR. No evidence found for shell execution, weak cryptography, network access, credential handling, prompt injection, or malicious intent. Residual risk is low because the skill includes executable robot protocol templates and a documented bundled-data file read example.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings occur in markdown inline code and fenced Python examples. The reviewed lines document Opentrons API calls and do not execute shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is markdown documentation or Python protocol examples, not Ruby code or shell execution. No subprocess, shell, eval, network, or command runner pattern was found during contextual review.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto, C2, and Reconnaissance Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static scanner matched lab automation terms, API method names, robot homing, PCR text, deck positions, and protocol metadata. These are normal Opentrons concepts and do not indicate cryptography, command and control, or system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged context is specific to Opentrons robot configuration and liquid handling. I found no hashing, encryption, malware coordination, host enumeration, or persistence behavior in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Executable Robot Protocol Templates Require Lab Validation
low_findings.description: The Python files are legitimate Opentrons protocol templates that can control pipettes, labware, and modules when run in the Opentrons environment. They are not malicious, but users should simulate and validate protocols before running physical robots.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scripts import the official Opentrons API and perform expected liquid handling and thermocycler actions. The risk is operational misuse or unsafe lab execution, not evidence of hostile code.

low_findings.title: Documented Bundled Data File Read Has Limited Scope
low_findings.description: The API reference shows reading a bundled data file through protocol.bundled_data. This is local file access for protocol inputs and no evidence of external transmission or arbitrary file traversal was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The example uses a bundled data path from the Opentrons protocol context, which is a legitimate workflow. It still represents filesystem access and should remain visible as a low-risk factor.

content.user_title: Create Opentrons Protocols for Lab Robots
content.value_statement: Opentrons protocol development requires precise API usage, labware choices, and robot-safe workflow design. This skill provides templates and reference guidance for Flex and OT-2 liquid handling automation.
actual_capabilities: Draft Protocol API v2 Python protocols for Opentrons Flex and OT-2 robots.
Configure pipettes, labware, deck slots, adapters, and hardware modules.
Build liquid handling steps with transfer, distribute, consolidate, mix, air gap, and touch tip operations.
Create PCR setup workflows with thermocycler lid, block temperature, and cycling profile steps.
Generate serial dilution and plate replication workflows for 96-well plate operations.
Explain Opentrons API methods, labware names, module methods, and simulation practices.
limitations: It does not connect to robots or run protocols directly.
Generated protocols still require simulation before physical robot use.
Deck layout, calibration, reagents, and lab safety checks remain user responsibilities.
It does not replace official Opentrons documentation for version-specific API changes.

use_cases.title: Automate PCR setup
use_cases.description: Create protocols that distribute master mix, add samples, and run thermocycler profiles.

use_cases.title: Prepare serial dilutions
use_cases.description: Build plate-based dilution workflows with clear volume, tip, and mixing choices.

use_cases.title: Prototype robot workflows
use_cases.description: Draft Flex or OT-2 protocols that combine labware, pipettes, modules, and simulation checks.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Transfer Protocol
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Opentrons Flex protocol that transfers 50 uL from A1 to B1 on a 96-well plate using a left-mounted p300 pipette.

prompt_templates.title: Serial Dilution Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Protocol API v2 serial dilution workflow across one row of a 96-well plate with a 1:2 dilution factor and mixing after each transfer.

prompt_templates.title: PCR Thermocycler Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Opentrons Flex PCR setup protocol for eight samples with master mix distribution, template addition, thermocycler lid control, and a three-step cycling profile.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Module Automation Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an Opentrons protocol that uses a heater-shaker, temperature module, and multi-channel pipette. Include deck layout, module sequencing, tip strategy, and simulation checks.

output_examples.input: Create a PCR setup protocol for eight samples on Flex.
output: Deck plan with thermocycler, tip racks, reagent rack, and PCR plate.
Master mix distribution and sample addition steps with suitable pipettes.
Thermocycler lid, temperature, cycling, final extension, and hold steps.
Simulation notes for checking labware names, volumes, and deck conflicts.

output_examples.input: Build a serial dilution protocol for a 96-well plate row.
output: Reservoir, plate, tip rack, and pipette setup guidance.
Diluent addition, stock transfer, repeated dilution, and mixing sequence.
Tip reuse strategy and warnings about volume limits and carryover.

output_examples.input: Review an Opentrons workflow before robot execution.
output: Checklist covering API level, robot type, labware names, deck slots, and module compatibility.
Simulation recommendations before upload to the robot.
Operational notes for calibration, reagents, and manual intervention points.

best_practices: Simulate every protocol before running it on a physical robot.
Use explicit labware labels, deck slots, pipette mounts, and API levels.
Add protocol comments for setup checks, manual pauses, and troubleshooting.
anti_patterns: Running generated protocols without simulation or human lab review.
Mixing Flex and OT-2 labware names, slots, or pipette definitions without checking compatibility.
Ignoring tip counts, well capacities, dead volumes, or contamination risk.

faq.question: Which robots does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers Opentrons Flex and OT-2 protocol patterns using Protocol API v2.

faq.question: Can it run a robot directly?
faq.answer: No. It helps create protocol files, but execution happens through Opentrons tools and robot software.

faq.question: Does it support hardware modules?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for temperature, magnetic, heater-shaker, thermocycler, and plate reader modules.

faq.question: Can it create PCR workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes PCR setup patterns with master mix, sample addition, lid control, and cycling profiles.

faq.question: What should I check before using a generated protocol?
faq.answer: Check robot type, API level, labware names, deck slots, pipette volumes, reagent volumes, and simulation output.

faq.question: Does it send lab data to external services?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The reviewed files contain documentation and local protocol templates without network calls.
